Title: Capture a new active treatment consent for a patient
Preconditions: Privacy officer logged in with consent management privilege; target patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient record | 2. Open the Consent panel | 3. Select scope Treatment | 4. Set status Active | 5. Set effective date to today | 6. Record patient signature method | 7. Save
Expected_Result: Consent is saved with a unique id, status Active, scope Treatment, recorded actor, and a timestamped creation audit entry

Title: Capture consent with multiple scopes in a single record
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent panel | 2. Add scope Treatment | 3. Add scope Research | 4. Add scope Disclosure to family | 5. Save | 6. Reopen record
Expected_Result: All three scopes persist independently and are each individually evaluable for authorization decisions

Title: Reject consent capture without a required scope
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent panel | 2. Leave scope unselected | 3. Set status Active | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a validation error indicating scope is mandatory; no partial record is persisted

Title: Reject consent with effective date after expiry date
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent panel | 2. Set effective date 2026-08-01 | 3. Set expiry date 2026-07-01 | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error: expiry must be on or after effective date; record not saved

Title: Boundary: expiry date equal to effective date
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent panel | 2. Set effective date 2026-07-01 | 3. Set expiry date 2026-07-01 | 4. Save | 5. Evaluate authorization at 2026-07-01T12:00
Expected_Result: Consent is accepted; it is considered active for the single calendar day per inclusive boundary rule

Title: Boundary: maximum future expiry horizon enforcement
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; policy max horizon = 10 years
Test_Steps: 1. Open Consent panel | 2. Set expiry to today + 10 years | 3. Save | 4. Repeat with today + 10 years + 1 day
Expected_Result: Expiry at exactly the max horizon saves; expiry beyond the max horizon is rejected with a policy validation error

Title: Capture consent on behalf of a minor by a verified guardian
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; minor patient with linked guardian relationship exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open minor patient record | 2. Open Consent panel | 3. Select grantor = guardian relationship | 4. Verify guardian identity attestation | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Consent records the guardian as the consenting actor with relationship captured; patient is referenced as the subject

Title: Block self-consent capture for a minor below age of capacity
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; minor patient (age 9) exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open minor record | 2. Attempt consent with grantor = patient self | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks self-consent for a patient under the configured age of capacity and requires a guardian grantor

Title: Decision table: authorization by scope and requesting purpose
Preconditions: Patient has consent scoped to Treatment only (active); requester role and purpose vary
Test_Steps: 1. Request access purpose=Treatment | 2. Request purpose=Research | 3. Request purpose=Marketing | 4. Record each decision
Expected_Result: Treatment purpose is permitted; Research and Marketing are denied because they fall outside the granted scope

Title: Pairwise: scope x actor-role x data-category access matrix
Preconditions: Multiple consent scopes configured; varying actor roles and data categories
Test_Steps: 1. Iterate combinations of scope {Treatment,Research}, role {Clinician,Researcher,Billing} and category {Clinical,Demographics,Financial} | 2. Evaluate each | 3. Log outcome
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the policy-correct permit or deny with no combination producing an undefined or default-allow result

Title: Deny access to a category explicitly excluded by consent provision
Preconditions: Patient consent permits Clinical but explicitly excludes Mental Health notes
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as treating clinician | 2. Request mental health note | 3. Observe decision and audit
Expected_Result: Mental health note access is denied by the except provision while general clinical access is allowed; denial is audited

Title: State transition: Active consent transitions to Expired at expiry
Preconditions: Consent active with expiry = today at 23:59
Test_Steps: 1. Verify access permitted before expiry | 2. Advance system clock past expiry | 3. Re-evaluate access | 4. Inspect consent status
Expected_Result: Status auto-transitions to Expired; subsequent access requiring that consent is denied; the transition is audited

Title: Boundary: access exactly at the expiry instant
Preconditions: Consent active with precise expiry datetime
Test_Steps: 1. Request access one second before expiry | 2. Request at the exact expiry second | 3. Request one second after
Expected_Result: Pre-expiry permitted; at-instant follows the documented inclusive/exclusive rule consistently; post-expiry denied

Title: Expired consent cannot be silently used after renewal lapse
Preconditions: A previously active consent has expired; no renewal recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt treatment data access relying on the expired consent | 2. Observe result
Expected_Result: Access is denied and the requester is informed consent has lapsed; no fallback to the expired grant occurs

Title: Revoke an active consent and confirm immediate effect
Preconditions: Patient has an active treatment consent; officer logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active consent | 2. Select Revoke | 3. Enter revocation reason | 4. Confirm | 5. Immediately re-evaluate access
Expected_Result: Status becomes Revoked with reason and timestamp; access depending on it is denied on the very next request

Title: State transition: Active to Revoked to no reactivation
Preconditions: An active consent exists
Test_Steps: 1. Revoke the consent | 2. Attempt to set status back to Active | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Reactivation of a revoked consent is blocked; a new consent record must be created instead, preserving the revoked record immutably

Title: Reject revocation without a recorded reason
Preconditions: Active consent exists; officer logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Open consent | 2. Select Revoke | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Revocation is blocked with a validation message requiring a reason for the audit trail

Title: Partial revocation removes one scope while retaining others
Preconditions: Consent with scopes Treatment and Research is active
Test_Steps: 1. Open consent | 2. Revoke only the Research scope | 3. Save | 4. Re-evaluate both scopes
Expected_Result: Research access is denied while Treatment access continues; the scope-level revocation is individually audited

Title: Break-glass emergency access grants temporary override with reason
Preconditions: Clinician without standing access; patient under emergency; break-glass enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt access and be denied | 2. Invoke Break-Glass | 3. Enter clinical justification | 4. Acknowledge override warning | 5. Access record
Expected_Result: Temporary access is granted, a high-visibility override banner is shown, and a break-glass audit event is written with actor, patient, reason and time

Title: Break-glass without justification is rejected
Preconditions: Break-glass enabled; clinician denied normal access
Test_Steps: 1. Invoke Break-Glass | 2. Leave justification blank | 3. Confirm
Expected_Result: Override is refused; justification is mandatory and no access is granted until provided

Title: State transition: break-glass session expires and access reverts
Preconditions: Active break-glass session with a fixed TTL
Test_Steps: 1. Establish break-glass access | 2. Wait until TTL elapses | 3. Attempt continued access | 4. Inspect status
Expected_Result: After TTL the elevated access ends and the clinician is denied again unless re-invoked; expiry is audited

Title: Break-glass event triggers mandatory post-hoc review flag
Preconditions: A break-glass access occurred
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a break-glass access | 2. Open the privacy review queue | 3. Locate the event
Expected_Result: The access appears in the privacy officer review queue flagged for mandatory post-hoc justification review

Title: Decision table: break-glass eligibility by role and patient state
Preconditions: Break-glass policy configured by role and emergency flag
Test_Steps: 1. Evaluate role {Physician,Nurse,Clerk} x patientEmergency {true,false} | 2. Attempt override per cell | 3. Record
Expected_Result: Clinical roles may break-glass; non-clinical roles such as Clerk are never eligible regardless of emergency flag

Title: Minimum-necessary returns only fields required for the stated purpose
Preconditions: Billing clerk requesting data for claims purpose
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as billing clerk | 2. Request patient record for purpose=Billing | 3. Inspect returned fields
Expected_Result: Only billing-relevant fields (demographics, coverage, charges) are returned; clinical notes and sensitive observations are withheld

Title: Negative: clinician cannot over-fetch beyond purpose scope
Preconditions: Clinician requests data citing a narrow purpose
Test_Steps: 1. Request with purpose=Referral | 2. Attempt to expand response to full chart | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Response is constrained to referral-relevant elements; attempts to widen the dataset beyond purpose are rejected and logged

Title: Pairwise: purpose x dataset-projection coverage
Preconditions: Multiple purposes and dataset projections configured
Test_Steps: 1. Iterate purpose {Treatment,Billing,Research,Audit} x projection {Full,Demographics,DeIdentified} | 2. Evaluate returned projection
Expected_Result: Each purpose maps to its least-privilege projection; Research receives DeIdentified by default unless explicit consent broadens it

Title: Every PHI disclosure writes an accounting-of-disclosures entry
Preconditions: Patient with consented disclosure to an external provider
Test_Steps: 1. Perform an authorized disclosure to an external provider | 2. Open accounting of disclosures | 3. Inspect the entry
Expected_Result: An entry records recipient, date, purpose, data categories, and the disclosing actor, satisfying accounting-of-disclosures requirements

Title: Disclosure log entries are immutable and tamper-evident
Preconditions: At least one disclosure entry exists; admin access available
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to edit a disclosure entry via UI | 2. Attempt direct datastore update | 3. Verify integrity hash/chain
Expected_Result: Edits are blocked; any out-of-band tampering is detected via integrity controls and surfaced as an alert

Title: Patient-facing accounting of disclosures excludes treatment-internal access
Preconditions: Patient requests an accounting of disclosures report
Test_Steps: 1. Generate the patient accounting report | 2. Inspect included and excluded events
Expected_Result: Report includes external and non-TPO disclosures and correctly excludes routine internal treatment access per regulation

Title: Database: disclosure record retention meets minimum period
Preconditions: Disclosure records older than the retention floor exist
Test_Steps: 1. Query disclosure table for oldest entries | 2. Verify retention age | 3. Run purge job | 4. Re-query
Expected_Result: Records younger than the retention floor are never purged; the purge job only removes entries beyond the regulatory window

Title: Restricted patient record is hidden from normal search results
Preconditions: Patient flagged as restricted/VIP; standard clinician without override logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the restricted patient by name | 2. Inspect search results
Expected_Result: The restricted patient does not appear in normal results, or appears masked without record access, per the restriction policy

Title: Authorized care-team member can access a restricted record
Preconditions: Patient flagged restricted; clinician is on the assigned care team
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as assigned care-team clinician | 2. Open the restricted record
Expected_Result: Care-team member is granted access, and the access is written to the audit log with the restriction context

Title: Negative: non-care-team access to restricted record is denied and alerted
Preconditions: Patient flagged restricted; clinician not on care team
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open the restricted record | 2. Observe denial | 3. Check privacy alerting
Expected_Result: Access is denied; a privacy alert is raised to the security/privacy team identifying the attempting actor

Title: State transition: applying and lifting a record restriction
Preconditions: Patient record currently unrestricted; privacy officer logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Apply restriction flag | 2. Confirm masked behavior | 3. Lift restriction with reason | 4. Confirm normal behavior
Expected_Result: Restriction applies and lifts correctly; both transitions are audited with actor and reason and take effect on next access

Title: Create a FHIR Consent resource and validate against profile
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint reachable; create privilege; patient resource exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Consent resource with status active and category, scope, patient, dateTime | 2. Read it back | 3. Validate against the profile
Expected_Result: Consent is created with 201, returns a logical id, echoes status active, and validates against the FHIR Consent profile

Title: Reject FHIR Consent missing the mandatory status element
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint reachable; create privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Consent resource omitting status | 2. Observe response
Expected_Result: Server returns 400 or 422 with an OperationOutcome indicating the required status element is missing; nothing is persisted

Title: FHIR Consent provision deny rule blocks a category read
Preconditions: A Consent resource with a deny provision on category=psychotherapy exists
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to read a psychotherapy note via FHIR | 2. Observe enforcement
Expected_Result: The read is denied or the resource is filtered out consistent with the deny provision; a permitted category remains readable

Title: FHIR Consent provision period boundary controls access window
Preconditions: Consent with provision.period start and end configured
Test_Steps: 1. Request access before period.start | 2. Within the period | 3. After period.end
Expected_Result: Access is denied outside the provision period and permitted within it, matching the period boundaries inclusively per spec

Title: Search FHIR Consent by patient and status
Preconditions: Multiple Consent resources exist for several patients
Test_Steps: 1. GET Consent?patient=Patient/123&status=active | 2. Inspect the bundle
Expected_Result: Only active consents for the specified patient are returned in a searchset bundle with correct total and self link

Title: Update FHIR Consent status to inactive reflects revocation
Preconditions: An active FHIR Consent resource exists
Test_Steps: 1. PUT the Consent with status=inactive | 2. Read it back | 3. Re-evaluate enforcement
Expected_Result: Resource version increments, status is inactive, and enforcement now denies access previously granted by that consent

Title: Authorization denies access when no consent exists for required scope
Preconditions: Patient has no consent for the Research scope; researcher logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as researcher | 2. Request research data | 3. Observe decision
Expected_Result: Access is denied because absence of consent defaults to deny (opt-in), and the denial is audited

Title: Conflicting consents resolve to the most restrictive outcome
Preconditions: Two consents exist: one permits, one denies the same category
Test_Steps: 1. Configure permit consent | 2. Configure deny consent for same category | 3. Request access | 4. Record decision
Expected_Result: Conflict resolves to deny (most-restrictive precedence); decision rationale is captured in the audit trail

Title: Authorization decision latency stays within SLA under load
Preconditions: Consent decision service deployed; load harness available
Test_Steps: 1. Drive concurrent consent-evaluation requests | 2. Measure p95 latency | 3. Verify no decisions error out
Expected_Result: p95 decision latency stays within SLA and no request returns a default-allow due to timeout or error

Title: API: consent enforcement on REST PHI endpoint
Preconditions: REST API reachable; bearer token for a researcher; patient lacks research consent
Test_Steps: 1. Call GET /patient/{id}/observations with researcher token | 2. Inspect status and body
Expected_Result: API returns 403 with a privacy-policy error and leaks no PHI in the body or error message

Title: Fail-closed when consent service is unavailable
Preconditions: Consent decision service is down or unreachable
Test_Steps: 1. Take the consent service offline | 2. Request PHI requiring a consent decision | 3. Observe behavior
Expected_Result: System fails closed and denies access rather than defaulting to allow; the outage and denials are logged

Title: Audit log captures every consent grant, change and revocation
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; consent operations performed
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a consent | 2. Modify it | 3. Revoke it | 4. Open the audit log and filter by patient
Expected_Result: Three distinct audit entries exist with actor, action, before/after where applicable, timestamp and source, in order

Title: Audit entries cannot be deleted by an application user
Preconditions: Audit entries exist; admin and standard accounts available
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to delete an audit entry as admin | 2. Attempt as standard user | 3. Verify persistence
Expected_Result: No application role can delete audit entries; deletion attempts are themselves logged

Title: HL7 PRD/consent segment maps disclosure recipient correctly
Preconditions: HL7 interface configured to emit disclosure messages
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a disclosure that emits an HL7 message | 2. Inspect provider/recipient segment mapping
Expected_Result: Recipient identity and purpose map to the correct HL7 segments/fields without dropping required consent context

Title: Read access to sensitive records is audited even when permitted
Preconditions: Sensitive record access permitted by consent; audit enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Authorized clinician reads a sensitive record | 2. Open audit log | 3. Locate the read event
Expected_Result: Even permitted reads of sensitive categories generate an audit read event with actor, patient, time and category

Title: Exploratory: probe consent UI for inconsistent state on rapid edits
Preconditions: Consent officer logged in; patient with an existing consent
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly toggle scopes and status | 2. Use browser back during save | 3. Open the record in two tabs and edit both | 4. Observe consistency
Expected_Result: No orphaned, duplicated or partially-saved consents result; concurrent edits are resolved deterministically with a clear conflict message

Title: Accessibility: restriction and consent banners are screen-reader announced
Preconditions: Screen reader active; restricted patient record
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the restricted record with a screen reader | 2. Trigger the restriction warning | 3. Listen for announcement
Expected_Result: The restriction/consent warning is exposed as an ARIA alert and announced; focus moves to the dialog and is trapped

Title: Accessibility: break-glass justification dialog is keyboard operable
Preconditions: Break-glass enabled; keyboard-only operation
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger break-glass with keyboard only | 2. Tab through justification field and confirm | 3. Submit with Enter
Expected_Result: The dialog is fully keyboard navigable with visible focus, labeled fields, and no keyboard trap, and submits successfully

Title: Database: consent-scope referential integrity on patient merge
Preconditions: Two patient records each with consents; a merge is performed
Test_Steps: 1. Record consents on both patients | 2. Merge the duplicate into the surviving record | 3. Query consents for the survivor
Expected_Result: All consents reassociate to the surviving patient without orphaned rows; conflicting scopes resolve most-restrictive and the merge is audited

Title: Pairwise: consent-status x record-restriction x role decisions
Preconditions: Consent statuses, restriction flags and roles configured
Test_Steps: 1. Iterate status {Active,Expired,Revoked} x restriction {On,Off} x role {CareTeam,Other} | 2. Evaluate access per cell
Expected_Result: Each cell yields the correct permit/deny; only Active consent with care-team role on a restricted record permits access

Title: Boundary: bulk export caps returned records to authorized count
Preconditions: Researcher authorized for a cohort of N consented patients
Test_Steps: 1. Request a bulk export | 2. Include one extra non-consented patient id | 3. Inspect output count
Expected_Result: Export contains exactly the consented records; the non-consented patient is excluded and the exclusion is logged

Title: Self-disclosure (patient requesting own data) is logged but not consent-gated
Preconditions: Patient portal identity verified; right-of-access request
Test_Steps: 1. Patient requests their own record via portal | 2. Observe access | 3. Inspect disclosure/access log
Expected_Result: Patient receives their own data under right-of-access without third-party consent gating; the access is still recorded in the log

Title: Cascade: revoking consent halts in-flight scheduled disclosures
Preconditions: An active consent has a scheduled future disclosure queued
Test_Steps: 1. Queue a future disclosure under the consent | 2. Revoke the consent | 3. Wait for the scheduled time
Expected_Result: The pending disclosure is cancelled when consent is revoked and does not execute; the cancellation is audited

Title: Security: consent decision cannot be bypassed via parameter tampering
Preconditions: API reachable; user with no research consent on target patient
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a denied research request | 2. Tamper purpose/scope params and resubmit | 3. Try alternate ids and verbs
Expected_Result: All tampered variants are denied or correctly scoped server-side; client-supplied purpose cannot override server policy and attempts are logged
